items.title: USDA lists SNAP ‘food restriction waivers’ that limit soda/candy purchases in multiple states
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page shows states implementing restrictions on certain items purchased with SNAP, including Texas (effective 04/01/26) restricting sweetened drinks and candy and Arkansas (07/01/26) restricting soda, certain juice drinks, energy drinks, and candy. The same USDA table lists additional states with target dates later in 2026 such as Tennessee (07/31/26) and Ohio (10/01/26).

items.title: SNAP retailer stocking standards update takes effect Nov. 4, 2026 (retailer rules, not shopper eligibility)
items.summary: USDA says that as of Nov. 4, 2026, SNAP-authorized retailers must comply with updated stocking standards for staple foods. USDA notes that butter, jerky, cheese dip, snack bars, and fruit spreads no longer count as staple foods for retailer authorization, but the rule does not change what customers can buy with SNAP benefits.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash benefit amounts ($26 kids; $48 pregnant/postpartum; $52 breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts for fruits and vegetables: $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA notes these are FY 2026 amounts, covering Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026.

items.title: SSA posts official 2026–2027 payment schedule (SSI on the 1st; Social Security on the 3rd for some; otherwise Wednesdays)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment schedule states that SSI is paid on the 1st of the month, and Social Security is paid on the 3rd for people who received Social Security before May 1997 or who receive both Social Security and SSI. For other Social Security recipients, SSA lists payments on the 2nd Wednesday (birthdays 1–10), 3rd Wednesday (11–20), or 4th Wednesday (21–31).

items.title: CMS guidance: starting Jan. 1, 2027, Section 1115 Medicaid waivers need Chief Actuary certification of budget neutrality
items.summary: CMS guidance says that beginning Jan. 1, 2027, the Secretary may not approve a Medicaid Section 1115 demonstration (or an amendment or renewal) unless the CMS Chief Actuary certifies it is not expected to increase federal Medicaid spending versus what would have happened without the demonstration. CMS also says if a final rule is not effective before Jan. 1, 2027, it expects to apply the approach provisionally to new demonstrations, amendments, and renewals until a final rule is effective.

items.title: IRS disaster tax relief: San Carlos Apache Tribe deadlines postponed to Sept. 28, 2026
items.summary: The IRS announced tax relief for individuals and businesses in the San Carlos Apache Tribe area affected by severe storms and flooding (Oct. 10–13, 2025), postponing various filing and payment deadlines to Sept. 28, 2026. The IRS notice says the postponement covers (among other items) individual income tax returns and payments, estimated tax payments, and quarterly payroll and excise tax returns normally due on Oct. 31, 2025; Jan. 31, 2026; Apr. 30, 2026; and Jul. 31, 2026.

items.title: USDA requests public comment on SNAP retailer incentive-waiver information collection (comments due July 27, 2026)
items.summary: USDA published a notice about an information collection renewal for SNAP waiver requests that allow retailers to offer incentives to SNAP recipients (to encourage healthier purchases) by waiving SNAP’s equal treatment provisions. The notice states written comments must be received on or before July 27, 2026.

items.title: USDA posts Privacy Act system-of-records notice for the National SNAP Information Database (USDA/FNS-15)
items.summary: USDA published a Privacy Act system-of-records notice for USDA/FNS-15, the National SNAP Information Database, describing its purpose to validate eligibility determinations and strengthen program integrity. The notice describes the system as owned, administered, and secured by USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service.
